notes: Singapore's IMDA produces AI governance frameworks including AI Verify (testing toolkit) and the Model AI Governance Framework.

match_nodes.description: AI Verify testing toolkit for responsible AI

versions_available.note: Second edition of the Model AI Governance Framework.

match_nodes.description: Model AI Governance Framework for Generative AI

data.official_name: Model AI Governance Framework for Generative AI
data.notes: Published by IMDA/AI Verify Foundation in 2024 as the second generation of Singapore's Model AI Governance Framework (first gen: Traditional AI, secid:control/imda.gov.sg/model-governance, 2020). Organized around 9 focus areas: accountability, data, trusted development/deployment, incident reporting, testing/assurance, security, content provenance, safety/alignment R&D, and AI for public good.

match_nodes.description: Model AI Governance Framework for Agentic AI

data.official_name: Model AI Governance Framework for Agentic AI
data.notes: Launched January 22, 2026 by the Ministry of Digital Development and Information (MDDI)/IMDA — described as the world's first governance framework specifically for autonomous AI agents. Structured around 4 pillars: bounding risk upfront, human accountability, technical controls across the agent lifecycle, and end-user transparency/training.

match_nodes.description: Project Moonshot — LLM red-teaming and evaluation toolkit

data.notes: Open-source LLM red-teaming/evaluation toolkit developed by IMDA's AI Verify Foundation, billed as the first open-source toolkit combining benchmarking and red-teaming for LLMs. Companion tool to AI Verify (secid:control/imda.gov.sg/ai-verify), used to operationalize testing under the GenAI/Agentic AI governance frameworks.

data.official_name: Specification for Multi-Tiered Cloud Computing Security (SS 584)
data.notes: Singapore Standard developed under the Information Technology Standards Committee (ITSC), overseen by IMDA, built on ISO/IEC 27001. World's first tiered cloud-security certification standard (2013, revised SS 584:2020). 3 certification tiers (535 controls total); mandatory for CSPs bidding on Singapore government tenders.
